generated_by: cellc audit-bundle --target-profile ckb (real compiler output, not hand-authored)
mapping_method: Direct comparison of each criterion against generated evidence: (a) proof_plan records, (b) codegen_coverage_status values, (c) locks[] array, (d) builder_assumptions, (e) source_units visibility, and (f) entry ABI/runtime access records. Source-level require/assert statements are recorded separately as source_guard_evidence and do not by themselves qualify a criterion as covered_by_generated_audit.
notes: This file is intentionally brutal. 'covered_by_generated_audit' is only used when the compiler's ProofPlan + coverage explicitly demonstrates the obligation for the active entry. Source guards are valuable but are not the same as generated ProofPlan obligations. The BTC authority lock is now visible in the generated locks[] surface for Script.args binding and spawn/IPC wiring; the staged RISC-V shell has local and child-verifier CKB VM BIP340 evidence, but the parent/child verifier decision is still not CKB-VM-transaction-covered.

overall_assessment: NovaSeal core now has split-intent receipt commitments, eight-fixture state/type and combined transaction evidence, and live local devnet RPC evidence for bootstrap -> key-auth transition plus wrong-signature rejection. It is still non-production: strict 0.16 generated ProofPlan fails on three runtime-required output/resource records, public/shared deployment pinning is missing, Molecule/wallet signing vectors are missing, and the BIP340 runtime verifier remains external TCB.

multi_script_reality.implication: The default generated ProofPlan exposes btc_authority, generic BTC BIP340 verifier wiring, checked IPC envelope/status records, and spawn target obligations. The state action has CKB VM fixture evidence for all eight fixtures, combined transaction evidence for all eight fixtures, and live local devnet stateful evidence for the core lifecycle. Remaining gaps are strict generated output/resource coverage, public/shared deployment pinning, Molecule/wallet signing alignment, and external review of the verifier TCB.

limits: shared witness ABI is exercised by state-type, parent-lock, and combined lock+type harnesses; it still does not prove public/shared deployment pinning or Molecule/wallet encoder alignment
Runs ckb-script TransactionScriptsVerifier::verify over eight constructed transactions containing the parent lock script, state type/action script, and staged child verifier cell_dep.
Uses the shared CSARGv1 witness payload for both lock and type/action execution.
The transactions are in-memory harness ResolvedTransaction values, not public/shared deployment-pinned transactions.

Builder-shape checks derive fee, occupied-capacity floor, under-capacity negative shape, and expected code dep roles from the constructed transaction and resolved deps, but still do not prove live dep liveness or mempool propagation.
Resolved code deps and input cells are deterministic harness cells, not live chain cells.
Negative fixture matching is outcome plus lock/type script-scope matching; it is not yet a full semantic error-code proof for every failure mode.
Receipt output data is materialised as Output#1 and checked by the state type/action script; public/shared deployment pinning and Molecule/wallet signing alignment remain out of scope.
